arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Bayern Munich’s Max Eberl not offering hints on pursuit of Jonathan Tah next summer; Manuel Neuer complimentary of FC Barcelona’s Marc-André ter Stegen; Joshua Kimmich on Barca’s shortlist; Bayern players honored; and MORE!
arts.sum: During an appearance on Sport1-Doppelpass over the weekend, Bayern Munich board member for sport Max Eberl was quizzed about the club’s rumored pursuit of Jonathan Tah over the summer, but would not reveal too much about what might happen going forward: Bayern Munich midfielder Joshua Kimmich is still rumored to be a favorite of FC Barcelona and Hansi Flick:

arts.ttl: Winter is coming: Bayern Munich expects clarity on futures of Leroy Sané, Joshua Kimmich and Alphonso Davies in next few months
arts.sum: The contracts of Thomas Müller, Manuel Neuer, Leroy Sané, Joshua Kimmich and Alphonso Davies all expire next summer. According to a report from kicker (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ), Bayern Munich is seeking to get a clear idea what the future holds for Leroy Sané, Joshua Kimmich, and Alphonso Davies by the winter time.

arts.ttl: Max Eberl compares Leon Goretzka’s potential path to another Bayern Munich success story
arts.sum: That task proved nearly impossible as highly compensated veterans like Serge Gnabry, Leon Goretzka, and Kingsley Coman refused to accept pay cuts or even transfer requests. On the other hand, Goretzka has literally played one minute as an emergency center back substitute.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich CEO says summer transfer strategy worked
arts.sum: Bayern Munich CEO Jan-Christian Dreesen is very happy with the new signings made by the club over the summer. That has worked very well,” said Dreesen.

arts.ttl: Cyler’s Fantastical FM24 Fixation: The story of Bayern Munich so far...
arts.sum: The big tragedy of this season really was Thomas Müller, who simply couldn’t keep fit and didn’t even make 20 appearances across the season, despite my needing his input numerous times during the year as Musiala and Sané cannot just play every minute. Losing Gnabry, especially for the fee Liverpool paid for him (€53m) was not a massive problem, but losing Sané definitely was as he was by far my best player this season, averaging a rating of 7.5 with 12 goals and 11 assists in the league alone by the end of the year.

arts.ttl: Is Bayern Munich head coach Vincent Kompany concerned by late Holstein Kiel attacking surge? “No”
arts.sum: When you make 5 substitutions in the second half, it can happen that sometimes you’re one step too late or you don’t defend some situations 100% as you did in the first half. Bayern Munich demonstrated an array of attacking power and interesting tactical set ups that allowed the team to steamrollHolstein Kiel, with the team 4-0 up at halftime and running up the scoreline to a 6-1 finish today.

arts.ttl: Paul Wanner on Bayern Munich’s superstars: “They welcomed me warmly”
arts.sum: After this season, Bayern Munich is going to welcome back Paul Wanner from his loan stint at FC Heidenheim, but the club once welcomed the youngster for the first time when he was just 16-years-old. I can just say that it was a really great time at the beginning and like I said, they welcomed me really warmly and it was a really good fit.”
